title: 08 anime effect reaches the game
prompt: Use case: ads-marketing. REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Image 1 locks Raul and Meg. Image 2 locks the wide geography. Image 3 supplies only the localized anime propagation across the football field. Image 4 locks Raul's mecha. BEAT — Wide view over Raul and Meg's shoulders from the top row. Mecha-Raul glows softly at frame right while a visible orange wave travels down the empty seats and reaches the live football play. Only the active play becomes anime: one runner gains controlled speed lines, pompoms pop into small orange graphic bursts, and the scoreboard emits a stylized pulse. The physical field and people remain recognizable. Meg watches the effect reach the game. AVOID — real fire, destruction, entire candyland replacement, empty play, anime teenagers in the foreground, illegible geography.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one polished cinematic live-action Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, contact sheet, or storyboard grid. Treat each attached image only according to its declared authority. Preserve Raul and Meg as the same teenagers, and preserve the established top-of-bleachers world, dusk light, clothing, and geography. Attached ingredient sheets are exact character or object design authorities; reproduce them faithfully rather than redesigning them from prose. Keep the surreal event localized and legible inside the live-action stadium. Candy remains tactile, translucent, appetizing, and benign. No anatomy, gore, generic gummy bears, captions, borders, watermarks, or invented package copy.
